title: Region-tagged variants (US NCI/COG vs EU ESMO/CCLG)
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. A backlog/future task (only after M5) producing region-tagged content variants (US NCI/COG vs EU ESMO/CCLG), each with its own per-region reviewer and sourcing. Drafts are marked 'draft' and not published until per-region dual sign-off. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Produce region-tagged content variants (US NCI/COG vs EU ESMO/CCLG), each region-sourced and region-reviewed, only after M5, pending per-region dual sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Region-tagged variants sourced from the region-appropriate vetted bodies (US: NCI/COG; EU: ESMO/CCLG), each assertion cited with provenance
Each region variant carries its own per-region reviewer (oncologist + advocate) sign-off; undertaken only after M5
Drafted strictly from provided vetted sources (no model-generated facts); marked draft, not published, until per-region dual sign-off
